currentState: CLI, integration, automation, retention, MCP, and workflow features are covered by tests.
Stable self-host assets should live in repo root and stay reviewable in git.
knownRisks: Real provider authentication may differ across local environments.
Dogfood repair uses provider-driven editing and should stay isolated in a temporary worktree.
Automation runtime and archive outputs must stay ignored while stable memory assets remain tracked.
currentFocus.summary: The repository is configured to self-host and exercise its own memory system.

suggestedNextActions: Run npm run dogfood:exercise after code changes.
Run npm run dogfood:repair when self-host drift or breakage is detected.
validationSnapshot.summary: Baseline validation was synthesized for self-host initialization.

gotchas.cause: Dogfood scripts rely on Codex or Claude availability unless overridden.
gotchas.correctPath: Set AGENT_MEMORY_DOGFOOD_PROVIDER or explicit AGENT_MEMORY_*_BIN values when exercising the repo.
gotchas.symptom: Self-host init or repair can hang or fail when local agent auth is missing.
gotchas.title: Dogfood provider auth is environment-dependent

nextSteps.done: The stable root assets reflect the current repository behavior.
nextSteps.start: Run npm run dogfood:init after major dogfood flow changes.
nextSteps.title: Keep the self-host baseline fresh
nextSteps.why: Repo-root memory and integration assets are now part of the product surface.

nextSteps.done: The latest dogfood report is green.
nextSteps.title: Use dogfood exercise before shipping big changes
nextSteps.why: The worktree canary catches drift, integration breakage, and repair regressions.

project.name: @agent-connect/memory
project.summary: Self-host dogfood baseline for the agent-memory repository.

architectureNotes: Canonical state lives under .agent-memory and projects into docs/agent-memory.
Automation, integration, MCP workflow, retention, and dogfood are all first-class product surfaces.
The repository now self-hosts its own memory and integration baseline.

denseSourceAreas.note: Coverage is broad and doubles as executable product spec.
